task_completion.description: Agent opened and read the design brief from the desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document viewer or text editor is open displaying the design brief from the desktop.

task_completion.description: Agent created a new IFC4 project in BlenderBIM/Bonsai.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender interface is visible with the Bonsai/BlenderBIM add-on active, showing a new project initialized with the IFC4 schema in the scene properties.

task_completion.description: Agent created an IfcBuildingStorey for spatial containment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender outliner or Bonsai spatial tree displays an IfcBuildingStorey entity.

task_completion.description: Agent modeled the required elements: walls, slab, and sanitary terminal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport or outliner shows multiple IfcWall objects, an IfcSlab object, and an IfcSanitaryTerminal object.

task_completion.description: Agent created an IfcElementAssembly to represent the modular bathroom pod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outliner or object properties panel shows an entity explicitly classed as IfcElementAssembly.

task_completion.description: Agent aggregated the walls, slab, and sanitary terminal into the IfcElementAssembly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outliner hierarchy shows the IfcWall, IfcSlab, and IfcSanitaryTerminal objects nested under or assigned to the IfcElementAssembly, indicating proper IFC aggregation.
The Blender save dialog, file browser, or window title bar shows the file being saved as 'prefab_bathroom_pod.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to model the pod.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with Blender's 3D viewport, outliner, and Bonsai properties panels, rather than a terminal window running a Python script to generate the IFC file.

integrity.description: Agent actually created the elements in Blender step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: A logical progression of modeling steps is visible across screenshots (e.g., adding walls, then slab, then assembly), rather than just opening a pre-existing completed file.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually write the IFC file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is using Blender/Bonsai for the modeling process, not typing IFC STEP physical file (SPF) syntax into a text editor.
